Title: Thai court accepts petition challenging government's US$12bil borrowing decree
Article:
BANGKOK: Thailand's Constitutional Court on Monday accepted a petition from the parliamentary opposition challenging the legality of a government emergency decree to borrow 400 billion baht (US$12.26 billion) to support the economy amid the fallout from the Middle East conflict.
The court in a statement said it has instructed the government to submit explanatory documents within seven days.
The government approved the loan decree earlier this month, with half of the borrowing for mitigating the cost of living, largely through a consumer subsidy scheme, and the other half for supporting a transition to clean energy.
Finance Minister Ekniti Nitithanprapas earlier said the decree remains in effect and can still move forward.
When filing the petition, opposition People's Party leader Natthaphong Ruengpanyawut said he supported the transition to clean energy, but "we do not agree with using an emergency borrowing decree."
The finance minister said on Monday that the impact of the war will be felt in the current quarter after Southeast Asia's second-largest  expanded 2.8pct on the year, more than expected, in the first quarter of 2026.--REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kedah BN weighs going solo too
Article:
SIK: Kedah Barisan Nasional (BN) has not ruled out the possibility of contesting solo in the next election, following Johor BN's recent move ahead of its own state polls.
"I see that as a good example for us to follow in the future," Kedah BN chairman Datuk Seri Mahdzir Khalid told reporters after officiating the Keda Village land grant handover ceremony for the Sik district at Desa Keda Sungai Semiar here today.
"If Johor can propose it, Kedah can also do the same. However, any party decision must be made collectively with the top leadership, not individually. So, whatever decision is reached, we will refer to a collective decision when the time comes," he said.
Mahdzir added that Johor BN came to its decision to contest solo in all 56 state seats after weighing various factors.
"They have their justifications and they have weighed the pros and cons in making the decision, which is why they chose to move solo in all 56 seats," he said.
On Saturday, Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced that the coalition would field candidates in all 56 state seats in the upcoming state election, saying the decision was finalised after discussions with state Umno and BN grassroots leaders.
The move drew immediate flak from
Johor Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman
Aminolhuda Hassan, who said BN was sending a clear signal that the door to political cooperation in the state is now closed.
The escalating tension prompted a stern warning from PH chairman and prime minister, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, at the 2026 PH Convention yesterday.
Anwar said that
PH would retaliate
if any party attempted to betray or undermine the coalition, hinting at the possibility of dissolving parliament to hold an early 16th General Election (GE16) if coalition partners continued to destabilise the political landscape.
In response to the friction, Johor BN rejected claims today that its decision to contest solo amounts to a betrayal, insisting that participating in elections is a fundamental democratic right.
Johor BN secretary Datuk Abdul Halim Suleiman said attempts to portray the coalition's decision as an act of disloyalty were
misleading and unfair
. He stressed that Johor's political landscape differs from the federal arrangement, as the current state administration was formed based on the people's mandate during the 2022 Johor state election.
"The Johor state government today was formed based on the people's mandate in the 2022 state election, led by BN, with all state executive councillors coming from BN.
"Pakatan Harapan and Perikatan Nasional in Johor are not part of the state administration.
"They play the role of checks and balances in the state assembly, and that role continues to be respected," Abdul Halim said in a statement today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jealousy, unrequited love believed motive in woman's stabbing case
Article:
ALOR STAR: Jealousy and unrequited love are believed to have been the motives behind a man who allegedly stabbed a woman in the neck while inside a car in front of a restaurant along Jalan Titi Gajah near here last night.
Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said, however, that investigations were ongoing to establish the exact motive in the incident involving a 32-year-old suspect and a 35-year-old victim.
"Police received a call at 9.24pm reporting a fight in front of a restaurant. The female victim sustained a neck injury, believed to have been caused by a sharp object, and was found inside the car covered in blood.
"The suspect fled the scene immediately after the incident and was later
arrested
while hiding near a place of worship in Taman Uda, about 6km from the location," he said at a press conference at the Kedah police headquarters today.
Adzli said the victim, a single mother, and the suspect, a divorcee, had known each other for more than a year and both worked in the real estate sector.
"The incident is believed to have happened shortly after they arrived at the restaurant. Both had not yet exited the vehicle when the suspect got out of the victim's car and fled towards a nearby padi field.
"The victim's quick action in sounding the car horn attracted public attention, and initial assistance was provided to stem the bleeding.
"The stab wound is believed to have been inflicted from the left side of the neck and penetrated through to the right. The weapon used could have been an ice pick, screwdriver or a Rambo knife," he said.
[[nid:1442541]]
He added that the victim was now in stable condition and that statements will be recorded from her and several witnesses to assist investigations.
Adzli said the suspect had a prior criminal record under Section 326 of the Penal Code for causing grievous hurt to his former girlfriend several years ago.
"Urine screening returned negative for drugs, and the suspect has been remanded for seven days until May 24. The case is being investigated under Section 326 of the Penal Code," he said.
Earlier, it was reported that the suspect had been remanded for seven days until May 24 to assist in the investigation of a stabbing case involving a woman in a car here.
Magistrate Noor Shifa Mohd Hamzah issued the
remand
order at the Alor Star court lockup.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 99 Speed Mart posts higher net profit of RM188.56mil in Q1
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: 99 Speed Mart Retail Holdings Bhd posted a higher net profit of RM188.56 million in the first quarter of its financial year ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 2026), compared with RM144.90 million in the same period last year.
In a Bursa Malaysia filing today, it said the performance was driven by an improved non-essential product mix and effective energy-saving measures.
The revenue jumped to RM3.07 million in Q1 2026 compared to RM2.61 million in Q1 2025, attributed to the continued expansion of its outlet network, with a net addition of 253 outlets year-on-year, bringing the total outlet count to 3,086 as of March 31, 2026.
"Revenue growth was also supported by the implementation of earlier operating hours nationwide. Accordingly, total sales transactions increased 18.0 per cent to 141.1 million, while the average basket size remained stable at RM21.70," it said.
The company also declared a first interim dividend of 2.25 sen per ordinary share, amounting to a payout of approximately RM189 million for the financial year ending Dec 31, 2026, with an entitlement date of June 5, 2026, and payment to be made on June 16, 2026.
Commenting on the company's performance, its chief executive officer, Lee Thiam Wah said the operations continue to benefit from resilient consumer demand, as they provide affordable daily necessities and accessible services to customers nationwide, in line with their Near 'n Save ethos.
He also said the group also continued to broaden their product offerings across the outlets -- including selected small electrical appliances -- to provide greater convenience and cater to the evolving needs of their customers.
"As consumer spending patterns continue to shift towards greater value consciousness, we will further strengthen our 'four items for RM10' promotional campaign and continue enhancing the 99 Bulksales platform to deliver better affordability and value to customers nationwide.
"At the same time, managing costs and improving utility efficiency remain key priorities for the group.
"We continue to implement various energy-saving initiatives, including the installation of solar panels across our retail and distribution operations and optimisation of lighting operating hours across our outlets, to enhance operational efficiency and support sustainable long-term growth," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran announces new body to manage Strait of Hormuz
Article:
TEHERAN: Iran's top security body announced on Monday the formation of a new body to manage the Strait of Hormuz, which Teheran has effectively closed and wants to charge ships to traverse.
On its official X account, the Supreme National Security Council shared a post for the Persian Gulf Strait Authority (PGSA) saying it would provide "real-time updates on the #Hormuz_Strait operations and latest developments."
The account of the Revolutionary Guards navy shared the same post.
It was not immediately clear what the new body would do but earlier this month Iranian English-speaking broadcaster Press TV said it constituted a "system to exercise sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz" and that ships passing through the strait were sent "regulations" from the email info@pgsa.ir.
Iran has largely blocked shipping through the vital strait since the outbreak of war with the United States and Israel on Feb 28. A fragile ceasefire has been in place since April 8.
Iran's grip over the waterway has rattled global markets and given Tehran significant leverage, while the United States has imposed its own naval blockade on Iranian ports.
In peacetime, the route accounts for roughly a fifth of global oil and liquefied natural gas shipments, along with other key commodities including fertiliser.
Since the war began, Iran has repeatedly said that maritime traffic through the strait would "not return to its pre-war status" and last month it said it had received the first revenue from tolls on the waterway.
On Saturday, Ebrahim Azizi, head of the Iranian parliament's national security commission, said Iran "has prepared a professional mechanism to manage traffic" through the strait, adding that it will be "unveiled soon."--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kedah cop linked to Langkawi drug syndicate transferred amid probe
Article:
ALOR STAR: A police officer believed to be the mastermind, known as "Mr A", in a drug trafficking network operating around Langkawi has been immediately transferred to another department, Kedah police confirmed.
Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said the transfer was not intended to protect the officer, but was carried out as investigations were still ongoing.
"We will take action even if the suspect is a member of the police force," he said at a press conference at the Kedah police headquarters today.
Adzli said the case required thorough investigations, including credible evidence and witness statements, before further action could be taken.
He also revealed that 54 police officers and personnel in Kedah had been arrested for drug abuse between 2023 and April this year.
Of that number, 38 have been charged in court and suspended from duty, while three others are still facing action.
In a related development, Adzli said police had also taken action against a 47-year-old man known as "Bagok", who was linked to a viral chart related to the so-called "Pablo Escobar 2.0" drug activities in Langkawi.
He said the suspect had been arrested twice this year, with the latest arrest on May 16.
"The first arrest was on April 11, when no drugs were found. However, an investigation paper was opened under Section 15(1) of the Dangerous Drugs Act after his urine test returned positive.
"More recently, he was arrested again three days ago and tested positive for methamphetamine," he said.
Adzli added last year, a police officer in Kedah had also been subjected to action under the Special Preventive Measures for suspected involvement in drug trafficking.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pos Malaysia's net loss narrows to RM19.5mil in Q1
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pos Malaysia Bhd's net loss narrowed to RM19.54 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, from a net loss of RM41.52 million in the same period last year, supported by stronger contributions across its business segments.
The postal and parcel services provider saw revenue growth of 7.3 per cent to RM501.35 million from RM467.06 million, driven mainly by its postal segment.
According to its Bursa Malaysia filing, the revenue from the postal segment rose 1.44 per cent to RM283.15 million from RM279.11 million, supported by higher contributions from the courier business, although this was partially offset by lower international mail revenue.
Its aviation, logistics, and other segments also recorded higher revenue for the quarter.
No dividend was declared for the period.
Pos Malaysia said it remains focused on reducing losses, supported by ongoing transformation initiatives including network rationalisation, digital channel expansion and disciplined cost management.
However, it said that the operating environment is expected to remain challenging due to structural industry pressures, competitive dynamics, and geopolitical uncertainties.
This also includes the developments in the Middle East, which may impact global freight rates, fuel costs, flight volumes, and air cargo demand relevant to its aviation segment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KLK's net profit jumps 91pct in Q2 boost by manufacturing segment
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd's (KLK) net profit jumped 90.6 per cent to RM294.05 million in the second quarter ended Mar 31, 2026 versus RM154.27 million in the corresponding period last year due to improved results from the manufacturing segment.
Revenue for the quarter under review climbed to RM6.55 billion from RM6.34 billion in the same quarter last year.
KLK said the improved performance was also attributable to land sales amounting to RM126.8 million.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said that its plantation segment posted a decline in profit by 21.1 per cent to RM358.5 million due to weaker selling prices of crude palm oil (CPO) and palm kernel.
These factors offset the higher sales volume in CPO and palm kernel as well as the reduction in CPO production cost.
The company said that it also posted a lower fair value loss of RM24.9 million from the valuation of unharvested fresh fruit bunches (FFB).
Its manufacturing segment recorded a wider loss of RM42.2 million in the second quarter despite higher revenue of RM5.7 billion.
"The weaker performance in the current quarter was mainly dragged down by a decline in profit contribution from the oleochemical division, partially mitigated by lower losses from the refineries and non-oleochemical divisions," it said.
The property  segment's profit fell 47.2 per cent to RM1.9 million on the back of lower revenue of RM25.7 million in the quarter under review.
For the six months ended Mar 31, 2026, the company posted a higher net profit of RM676.46 million from RM374.73 million. Revenue rose to RM12.9 billion from RM12.3 billion a year prior.
Looking ahead, KLK said performance for its plantation segment is expected to remain stable, supported by healthy production levels, notwithstanding the potential operational disruptions amid the conflict in the Middle East.
Margins across the manufacturing segment are also likely to remain competitive, amid elevated feedstock and utility costs.
"Supported by resilient plantation operations and improving downstream contributions, the group remains optimistic of delivering an improved operational performance for the financial year," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gaza-bound flotilla says Israeli forces intercepting fleet near Cyprus
Article:
JERUSALEM: Israeli forces were intercepting a Gaza-bound aid flotilla on Monday after it set sail from Turkey last week, with organisers saying one of the vessels was stopped west of Cyprus.
"Military vessels are currently intercepting our fleet and IDF forces are currently boarding the first of our boats in broad daylight," the Global Sumud Flotilla posted on X.
"We demand safe passage for our legal, non-violent humanitarian mission. Governments must act now to stop these illegal acts or piracy meant to maintain Israel's genocidal siege on Gaza.
"Normalisation of the occupation's violence is a threat to us all."
Around 50 ships had departed from southwestern Turkiye on Thursday as part of the flotilla.
On Monday, Israel had vowed to block the vessels.
"Israel will not allow any breach of the lawful naval blockade on Gaza," the foreign ministry posted on X.
"Israel calls on all participants in this provocation to change course and turn back immediately."
The ministry did not immediately respond to requests for comment on whether Israeli forces were intercepting the boats.
The Global Sumud Flotilla is the third initiative in a year aimed at breaking an Israeli blockade of Gaza, which has suffered severe shortages of food, water, medicine and fuel since the Israel-Hamas war broke out in October 2023.
Israeli authorities reject claims of aid shortages, saying the Palestinian territory is "flooded" with supplies.
Israeli forces intercepted the second flotilla in international waters off Greece on April 30, expelling most of the activists to Europe.
But they arrested two of them, who were held for several days in Israel before they were deported.
Rights groups said the arrests were illegal and that the men suffered abuse while in Israeli detention.
Israeli authorities have rejected the abuse allegations but filed no charges against the pair.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE PM MAY 18, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed lower on Monday, as selling pressure spread across key index-heavy stocks.
The benchmark index ended the session at 1,727 points, with sentiment weighed down by rising geopolitical uncertainty linked to the Iran conflict.
In the currency market, the ringgit weakened against the US dollar, closing at 3.9680.
Meanwhile, Bitcoin was steady at RM305,370,  while Ethereum remained stable at RM8,407
That wraps up today's Market Pulse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: TVET-AI programme to be rolled out in five SBP schools this year, says MoE
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Education Ministry (MoE) has formalised a strategic collaboration through a memorandum of understanding with Multimedia University and Huawei Technologies (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd to strengthen Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) in artificial intelligence (AI) at fully residential schools.
The ministry said the TVET-AI initiative would be implemented this year in five selected fully residential schools before being expanded nationwide.
The schools involved are Sekolah Menengah (SM) Sains Banting in Selangor, SM Sains Setiu in Terengganu, SM Sains Alor Gajah in Melaka, SM Sains Kuching in Sarawak, and SM Sains Tun Syed Sheh Shahabudin in Penang.
It said the collaboration reflected its ongoing commitment to education reform aimed at strengthening academic excellence alongside future-ready technical skills through the integration of AI.
"This strategic initiative adds significant value to the ministry's efforts to enhance student employability through the Huawei Certified ICT Associate – Artificial Intelligence (HCIA-AI) professional certification, which is internationally recognised," it said in a statement.
The ministry said the programme was aligned with the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP) and the Malaysian Education Blueprint 2026–2035, which emphasise future talent development, strengthening the TVET ecosystem and enhancing industry-academia collaboration. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sunway Construction's Q1 net profit up 56pct to RM118.41mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sunway Construction Group Bhd's net profit for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26) surged by 56 per cent to RM118.41 million compared with RM75.71 million in the same quarter last year, underpinned by higher contributions from all operating segments.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, it said revenue, however, decreased to RM1.02 billion from RM1.40 billion previously.
The group declared a total dividend of 22.8 sen per share in the current quarter for the financial year ending Dec 31, 2026, comprising a single-tier first interim dividend of 7.60 sen per share and a special dividend of 15.2 sen per share.
Its managing director Liew Kok Wing said the group's healthy outstanding order book, coupled with robust order book replenishment, continues to provide strong visibility for its earnings growth trajectory.
"In Q1 2026, the group secured RM3.59 billion worth of new orders, achieving more than 50 per cent of its 2026 order book replenishment target," he said in a separate filing.
On prospects, he said the group continues to strengthen its position in Malaysia's key growth sectors, particularly the advanced technology facilities (ATF) segment, while maintaining a diversified construction order book profile across various sectors, including Sunway Group's projects.
Within the ATF segment, the group secured three new data centre projects and onboarded a new international hyperscale operator in Q1 2026, further strengthening its multinational technology client base, he said.
"Sunway Construction has delivered more than 180 megawatt (MW) of data centre capacity to date and is currently managing 10 ongoing projects for global technology clients," said Liew.
He added that the group remains cautiously optimistic for the remainder of 2026, supported by its healthy and diversified outstanding order book of RM8.157 billion.
It remains vigilant amid evolving geopolitical developments and the spillover effects from cost escalation and supply chain disruptions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Aeon posts higher net profit of RM83.27mil in Q1
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Aeon Co (M) Bhd posted a higher net profit of RM83.72 million in the first quarter of its financial year ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26), compared with RM68.10 million in the same period last year.
The general merchandise store and supermarket chain stated in a Bursa Malaysia filing today that the improvement was attributed to a more favourable product mix and higher variable income from the property management services segment.
This increase was driven by heightened festive shopping activity, supported by effective cost management and enhanced operational discipline.
Revenue inched down to RM1.242 billion in Q1 FY26, from RM1.244 billion in Q1 FY25, driven by effective cost management and improved operational efficiency.
Aeon managing director Tsugutoshi Seko said the company remain focused on strengthening its operational fundamentals and advancing key growth drivers.
"It would include improving efficiency, expanding their private brands offerings and enhancing customer engagement through their loyalty programme," he said.
At the same time, the group will continue to advance our sustainability initiatives to support longterm value creation.
"While ongoing global uncertainties may continue to influence consumer sentiment and spending patterns, we expect underlying demand to remain resilient, supported by steady spending on essential goods, sustained in-mall activity and supportive government stimulus initiatives," said Seko.
He noted that the group remains focused on executing its strategic priorities, navigating evolving challenges and delivering sustainable long-term value to our shareholders.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Capital A net profit drops 96pct in Q1 due to forex loss
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Capital A Bhd logged a lower net profit of RM22.45 million for the first quarter (Q1) ended March 31, 2026,  down 96.7 per cent compared with RM689.57 million in the same period a year earlier.
The decrease was mainly due to a RM2 million foreign exchange loss in the first quarter, compared with a RM21 million foreign exchange gain in the corresponding quarter of 2025.
It also reflected the cessation of RM15 million in interest income from discontinued operations following the debt settlement upon completion of the disposal.
Despite the lower earnings, Capital A reported higher revenue, rising 84.9 per cent to RM766.59 million from RM414.52 million in the corresponding quarter, according to its Bursa Malaysia filing.
Capital A said total segmental revenue before eliminations remained broadly stable year-on-year, with a slight decline to RM775 million from RM778 million in the same period last year.
It added that the group recorded improved operational momentum in Q1, supported by the expansion of its core businesses, including travel, logistics, maintenance, repair and overhaul, and ancillary travel-related segments.
No dividend was declared for the quarter under review.
Capital A said it has experienced minimal impact from the Middle East conflict.
"However, management recognises the correlation between the group's performance and the core airline business
"Anticipating potential capacity cuts, stop-gap measures—such as increasing fares, meal prices, and introducing a fuel surcharge—have been implemented to protect commission collection and meal supply. Capital A remains vigilant and continues to monitor market conditions," it added.
Capital A said the second quarter is typically a quieter period due to the low travel season, but expects a strong pickup in the following quarter with demand peaking in the fourth quarter.
"This growth will be supported by the e-commerce peak boosting the logistics business (Teleport) and heightened travel demand driving performance for AirAsia MOVE, Santan, and AirAsia Next," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Farmiera posts RM3mil in net profit for Q1
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Poultry producer and distributor Farmiera Bhd logged a net profit of RM3.07 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026.
The company recorded revenue of RM167.20 million for the quarter, comprising RM87.17 million from poultry farming and RM80.03 million from poultry processing, according to its Bursa Malaysia filing.
It posted a profit before tax of RM4.49 million and a profit after tax of RM3.07 million for the period.
"The performance was supported by balanced contributions from both its poultry farming and poultry processing segments, which accounted for 52.14 per cent and 47.86 per cent of total revenue respectively," it said in a statement.
Farmiera managing director and chief executive officer Hong How Seng said that following the company's listing on the ACE Market last year, it remains committed to strengthen market position through upstream vertical integration to improve margins and enhance operational efficiency.
He said the strategy is progressing well, as shown by the completion and commencement of operations at four parent stock farms.
Hong added that the remaining two farms are expected to be completed in the second and fourth quarters of 2026, respectively.
"Looking ahead, we remain cautiously optimistic about our prospects, supported by ongoing government initiatives to strengthen food security, alongside the continued execution of our expansion plan," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Showdowns and spycraft on Trump-Xi summit sidelines
Article:
WHILE Donald Trump and Xi Jinping were hailing their friendship for the cameras, it was less amicable for the rival Chinese and US security services.
From a standoff over a US Secret Service agent's weapon to a US staffer trampled during a melee, tensions bubbled to the surface throughout Trump's visit to Beijing.
The mutual distrust was underscored when US officials made staffers and media, including an AFP reporter, boarding Air Force One hand over all badges and pins given to them by the Chinese side.
Along with "burner" phones from the US delegation, the confiscated items were tossed in a trash can at the bottom of the stairs of the presidential jet.
For all Xi's warm words about being "partners not rivals," this was always a summit between two superpowers with a long history of suspicion.
As they vie for supremacy without tipping over into conflict, both sides are taking their espionage and counterintelligence efforts to new heights.
The result in Beijing verged at times towards collective paranoia.
US officials and journalists travelling to China to cover the summit were advised to use phones and laptops that could be wiped or disposed of back home to preserve cybersecurity.
The frictions also played out on the ground in Beijing as US and Chinese officials appeared to be having their own shadow superpower battle.
Two incidents made headlines, including when Chinese officials blocked US officials and journalists in a room during Trump and Xi's visit to the Temple of Heaven.
"We're in the motorcade with the president. Do you not understand that?" said one journalist, in scenes witnessed by an AFP reporter.
A Chinese official replied: "The security of our side does not allow you."
Voices were raised and the US group ended up pushing past the Chinese team to get to their vans in Trump's motorcade before it left.
"US press, we are going," a US official said. "Be gentle but we are going. Don't run over anybody, do not do what they did to us."
That appeared to refer to an earlier incident at the Great Hall of the People, in which a US staffer fell and had her ankle trodden on as officials from both sides tried to corral journalists rushing to film the start of the Trump-Xi talks.
Earlier at the temple, US and Chinese officials had clashed over whether a US Secret Service agent should be allowed to bring his weapon into the complex.
For nearly half an hour under the sun on Beijing's hottest day of the year, they had increasingly intense discussions as unyielding Chinese officials insisted the agent could not pass.
At almost every turn, there appeared to be diplomatic arm-wrestling between US and Chinese over timings, positions and alleged last-minute schedule changes.
US and Chinese officials like to talk about the principle of "reciprocity" in their relationship, particularly when it comes to confidence building measures.
But it can cut both ways – and Xi is now due to visit the White House in September.
At one point in the Temple of Heaven standoff, an official could be heard talking about what would happen on that trip.
Both sides are known for ensuring that when limitations are placed on numbers for officials or journalists, tit-for-tat measures are taken when the other country makes a return visit.
One senior US official in the administration of president Joe Biden was even reportedly denied breakfast after their counterpart did not get their morning meal.
With Trump and Xi eyeing three more meetings in 2026, it could be a long year for their staff.
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KPDN busts diesel syndicate abusing fleet cards in Penang raid
Article:
NIBONG TEBAL: The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) has crippled a syndicate suspected of misusing fleet cards to siphon subsidised diesel during a raid on a licensed wholesaler's premises here today.
The integrated operation under Ops Tiris 4.0 was carried out jointly with the Special Branch (E5) of the Penang police contingent headquarters following three weeks of intelligence gathering and surveillance.
Penang KPDN director S. Jegan said initial inspections at the premises uncovered activities involving the misappropriation of subsidised diesel.
He said authorities also seized 13 fleet cards believed to have been used in the operation.
"Investigations found that the premises was used as a transfer point for diesel from prime movers, which are believed to have repeatedly purchased fuel from nearby petrol stations using fleet cards," he said at the scene.
Enforcement officers also seized three prime movers, pumps, closed-circuit television (CCTV) equipment, purchase receipts and several business documents to assist investigations.
Authorities discovered about 10,600 litres of suspected diesel stored in five skid tanks and the original tanks of the prime movers, with the controlled goods valued at about RM53,000. The total value of the seizure was estimated at RM205,000.
Jegan said seven local men were detained to assist investigations.
The case is being investigated under Regulation 3(1) of the Control of Supplies Regulations 1974 for selling controlled goods without a licence, and Regulation 11 for purchasing controlled goods from unauthorised sources.
He said investigations were ongoing to identify individuals and companies involved in the diesel distribution chain, including the source of supply.
"Those convicted under the Control of Supplies Act 1961 face fines of up to RM1 million for the first offence and up to RM3 million for subsequent offences for individuals, or imprisonment of up to three years, or both.
"Companies may be fined up to RM2 million for the first offence and up to RM5 million for subsequent offences," he said.
KPDN would continue to strengthen enforcement and monitoring efforts, Jegan said, warning that strict action will be taken against any misuse of fleet cards for subsidised fuel diversion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gaza aid flotilla says Israeli forces intercepted 10 boats, contact lost with 23 vessels
Article:
ISTANBUL: The organisers of a flotilla of aid vessels bound for Gaza said on Monday that Israeli forces had intercepted 10 of their boats and that contact had been lost with a total 23 vessels in the eastern Mediterranean.
Earlier on Monday, Israel's foreign ministry had said on X that it "will not allow any breach of the lawful naval blockade on Gaza."
Ships from the Global Sumud Flotilla had set sail for a third time on Thursday from southern Turkiye, after earlier attempts to deliver aid to Gaza were intercepted by Israel in international waters.
Live video showed military vessels approaching the vessels on Monday.
"Military vessels are currently intercepting our fleet and (Israeli) forces are boarding the first of our boats in broad daylight," the Global Sumud Flotilla initially said on X.
"We demand safe passage for our legal, non-violent humanitarian mission."
The group said 10 boats had been intercepted and contact lost with 23 of the 54 vessels in the flotilla, naming some two dozen Turks among those on the intercepted vessels, some 250 nautical miles (463 km) from Gaza. It said there were 426 people taking part in the flotilla from 39 countries.
Israel's foreign ministry also called on "all participants in this provocation to change course and turn back immediately."
The previous flotilla departed from Spain on April 12. But Israeli forces intercepted vessels in that group, taking more than 100 pro-Palestinian activists to Crete and detaining two others in Israel.
Last October, Israel's military halted another flotilla assembled by the same organisation, arresting Swedish activist Greta Thunberg and more than 450 participants.
Palestinians and international aid bodies, along with Turkiye and a number of other countries, say supplies reaching Gaza are still insufficient, despite a ceasefire reached in October that included guarantees of increased aid.
Most of Gaza's more than 2 million people have been displaced, many now living in bombed-out homes and makeshift tents pitched on open ground, roadsides, or atop the ruins of destroyed buildings.
Israel, which controls all access to the Gaza Strip, denies withholding supplies for its residents.
Its foreign ministry said more than 1.58 million tonnes of humanitarian aid and thousands of tons of medical supplies have entered Gaza since October 2025.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ampang PKR leader urges Rodziah to quit seat over Rafizi-linked event
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: An Ampang PKR divisional leader has called on Ampang MP Rodziah Ismail to vacate her parliamentary seat and resign from the party following her attendance at an event linked to former PKR deputy president Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli in Petaling Jaya yesterday.
Ampang PKR deputy chief Daing Reduan Bachok said Rodziah's recent actions had triggered concern among grassroots members and party supporters.
He said any move that undermined party unity, eroded public confidence or contradicted the Reformasi struggle was unacceptable.
"The recent actions and stance of Ampang MP Rodziah Ismail have caused significant unease among members and supporters at the grassroots level.
"On the basis of the dignity of our struggle and respect for the people's mandate, I strongly urge Rodziah Ismail to immediately resign and vacate the Ampang parliamentary seat, as she no longer reflects the spirit of the struggle or loyalty to the party leadership," he said in a statement today.
Daing Reduan said Ampang voters had not given PKR and Pakatan Harapan a mandate for internal discord or factional politics.
"The people of Ampang gave their mandate to PKR and PH not to see the party weakened by internal political manoeuvring or the actions of those who fail to remain loyal to the party's cause.
"Ampang PKR wishes to stress that the grassroots here remain firmly behind the party president (Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim).
"We will not compromise on any action that could jeopardise party stability or erode public confidence in the Reformasi struggle," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: WCT wins RM152.68mil Taiwan highway interchange contract
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: WCT Holdings Bhd has secured a RM152.68 million contract to undertake construction works for a highway interchange project in Taiwan.
In a Bursa Malaysia filing today, the company said the Taiwan branch of its wholly owned subsidiary, WCT Bhd, was awarded the contract by WU En De Construction Co Ltd on May 18, 2026.
It said the contract was awarded to WCT Berhad Taiwan Branch, comprising construction works between the Gaoke Interchange and Gangshan Interchange under the "National Highway No. 1 - Addition of Gangshan Second Interchange Project".
"The works under the contract involve road works, bridge structural works and drainage works," said the engineering, construction and property development company.
The project is expected to be completed within 42 months from the contract commencement date of Aug 1, 2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Taiwan says would 'welcome' its president speaking to Trump
Article:
TAIPEI: Taiwan would "welcome" an opportunity for its leader to speak to US President Donald Trump after he raised the possibility, a senior Taiwanese diplomat said Monday.
Trump told reporters on Friday that he had to speak to the man "running Taiwan" – an apparent reference to President Lai Ching-te – about arms sales.
A conversation between Lai and Trump would be a major break in US diplomatic policy and risk a rupture with China, which claims Taiwan is part of its territory.
Trump made the remarks to reporters aboard Air Force One on the way back to Washington after a summit in Beijing where Chinese President Xi Jinping had pushed him not to support Taiwan.
"I'm going to make a determination. I'm going to see," Trump said in response to a question about whether he would go ahead with arms sales to Taiwan.
"I have to speak to the person that right now is – you know who he is – that's running Taiwan."
Taiwan Deputy Foreign Minister Chen Ming-chi said the government was seeking confirmation from the United States about whether Trump wanted to speak to Lai.
"We will use all channels to understand what the US side is actually thinking," Chen told reporters.
"We would of course very much welcome it," he said.
"If they are willing to talk with our president, I think that would be a very good opportunity and also a rare opportunity."
In 2016, shortly after his first election victory, Trump accepted a phone call from then Taiwanese president Tsai Ing-wen, angering Beijing.
It was the first call between a Taiwanese leader and an incoming or serving US president since Washington switched recognition from Taipei to Beijing in 1979, and it stunned diplomats, world leaders and China watchers.
Trump has caused jitters in Taipei after he suggested arms sales to Taiwan could be used as a bargaining chip with China, which has threatened to seize the island by force.
Lai and others in his government have pushed back, insisting such sales are part of Washington's security commitment to the island.
Taiwan was at the "core" of global interests and "will never be sacrificed or traded away", Lai said in a statement on Sunday.
The United States is required under US law to provide weapons to Taiwan for its defence, but it has been ambiguous on whether US forces would come to its aid.
Chen said Trump had created "some anxiety" in Taiwan and the government was using "all the communication channels to understand the real meaning behind" his remarks.
"So far our understanding is there is no change even in the arms sale," Chen said, describing arms sales as the "cornerstone" of peace and stability across the Taiwan Strait.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cuckoo's earnings drops in Q1 on softer demand
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Cuckoo International (MAL) Bhd posted lower net profit in the first quarter ended Mar 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26) due to softer consumer demand.
Earnings for the quarter under review slipped 10.1 per cent year-on-year to RM25.03 million from RM27.86 million a year ago.
Revenue dropped 23.1 per cent to RM227.83 million in Q1 FY26 from RM296.28 milliom in the same period last year as the company saw lower units sold in the quarter.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company noted that distribution and administration costs dropped 7.3 per cent to RM18 million and 14.5 per cenr to RM15.7 million, respectively.
The reduction in these expenses are mainly due to lower marketing expenses because of focused marketing spending.
Net losses on impairment of financial instrument rose 47.7 per cent to RM18.5 million, reflecting slower repayment behavior among spending shifted towards prioritising expenses in preparation for the Chinese New Year and Hari Raya celebrations in the first quarter.
Looking ahead, the the company remains cautiously optimistic on its long-term prospects.
"With consumers increasingly favouring rental arrangements as an alternative to outright purchases, the group is well-positioned to capitalise on this trend.
"Through continued investment in technology and wellness-focused home living solutions, including home appliances and household goods, home care services and other lifestyle solutions, the group is committed to sustain growth and build a resilient foundation for future growth," it added.
Cuckoo's share price closed unchanged on Monday at 43 sen, valuing the company at RM616.1 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Oil prices stay elevated amid geopolitical risk premium, says MARC Ratings
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Global oil prices are holding at elevated levels due to ongoing geopolitical tensions, even though supply still exceeds demand in the market, according to Malaysian Rating Corporation Bhd (MARC Ratings).
Its chief economist and adjunct professor, Dr Ray Choy, said prolonged tensions in the region have raised concerns over possible oil supply disruptions after damage to key energy facilities and pipelines, with repairs expected to take at least six months.
"There are oil pipelines that have been shut and cemented to prevent spillovers, in addition to various logistical issues that continue to disrupt the global energy supply chain.
"Besides the issue of facility repairs, the cost of transporting oil has also increased, as well as oil shipment insurance costs, which are now significantly higher compared to before the conflict," he said during the MARC360 special edition webinar today.
Choy said that over the next year, oil-importing countries are expected to increase their strategic reserves to build buffers in case geopolitical tensions escalate again.
He said this could help sustain oil demand at elevated levels even though the global market is fundamentally in a state of oversupply.
As such, Choy noted that global crude oil prices are expected to remain in the range of US$80 to US$90 per barrel in the near term.
He noted that this will be driven by ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East that continue to disrupt global energy supply chains and raise logistics and shipping security costs.
He said that while the global oil market remains structurally oversupplied, prices are being kept elevated by an ongoing geopolitical risk premium.
"Without the geopolitical risk premium, the global oil market would actually be in a state of oversupply.
"However, the ongoing conflict is keeping prices high.
"In the past, oil prices were in the range of US$60 to US$70 per barrel, but the current situation is different as the market is factoring in geopolitical risks that have yet to subside," he added.
On the regional economic outlook, Choy said countries that rely heavily on energy imports such as Thailand and the Philippines are expected to be more affected by the Middle East conflict compared to Malaysia.
He said a key issue to watch is the extent to which a slowdown in regional trade across Asia will impact Malaysia's net export position.
"Beyond trade, the tourism sector also needs to be monitored as higher airfares and more cautious consumer sentiment could weigh on tourism demand," he said.
He added that inflationary pressure is also expected to be the biggest challenge for the economy in the second quarter of the year, as rising producer costs begin to be passed on to consumers.
He said the key question is how far consumers are able to sustain their spending once cost pressures become more widespread.
"Some of the cost pressure will eventually be passed on to consumers, and the question is whether consumers can continue to spend or begin to cut back.
"However, a still-strong labour market and low unemployment rate are expected to help support consumer resilience," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia, India to deepen ties under Comprehensive Strategic Partnership, says Anwar
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia will continue to strengthen its close ties with India for mutual benefit, regional stability and more inclusive prosperity for both nations, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
Anwar said the matter was discussed during a farewell courtesy call by India's High Commissioner to Malaysia, B.N. Reddy, who will soon conclude his tenure in the country.
He said the meeting further reinforced Malaysia-India bilateral relations, now elevated to a Comprehensive Strategic Partnership, reflecting both countries' commitment to expanding cooperation across multiple sectors.
The prime minister said areas of cooperation included trade, digitalisation, artificial intelligence (AI), food security, education and people-to-people exchanges.
"I also touched on the positive development in bilateral trade, which reached US$18.59 billion in 2025, maintaining India as Malaysia's largest trading partner in South Asia," he said in a Facebook post today.
Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said cooperation in digital and emerging technologies had shown encouraging progress, including through the inaugural Malaysia-India Digital Council (MIDC) meeting and the MY-India AI Conclave recently held in Kuala Lumpur.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd
